Progress 进度20%
HOOK · Element / Compound / Mixture
元素 / 化合物 / 混合物
HOOK
•
Everything around you is one of three things: a pure element, a compound, or a mixture.
你身边所有东西,都是三种之一:纯元素、化合物、或混合物。
•
Pure copper is an element.
纯铜是元素。
•
Water is a compound.
水是化合物。
•
Air is a mixture.
空气是混合物。
•
Look at a glass of salt water, a copper coin, and the air above them — a mixture, an element, and a mixture.
看一杯盐水、一枚铜币、和它们上方的空气 —一个混合物、一个元素、一个混合物。
LEARN · Element / Compound / Mixture
元素 / 化合物 / 混合物
LEARN
Element = one type of atom
•
An element contains only one type of atom and cannot be broken down into anything simpler by chemical means.
元素只含一种原子,用化学方法不能再分成更简单的东西。
Compound = atom types chemically joined
•
A compound is a pure substance made of two or more types of atom chemically joined together, e.g.
化合物是纯物质,由两种或更多种原子化学结合在一起,例如水(H2O)、氯化钠(NaCl)。
•
water (H2O), sodium chloride (NaCl).
Mixture = not joined, physically separable
•
A mixture is two or more substances that are NOT chemically joined.
混合物是两种或更多种物质,没有化学结合在一起。
•
They can be separated by physical means and have no fixed proportion, e.g.
可以用物理方法分开,没有固定比例,例如空气、海水。
•
air, seawater.
🔴 RED CARD · 误区红牌
✘
Calling a mixture a compound just because particles are close together, or calling sodium chloride a mixture.
✔
Compound = atoms chemically JOINED (NaCl). Mixture = NOT joined, separable physically (seawater). WHY IT "joined vs not joined" is the deciding test; getting it backwards loses the mark. ZH 错: 因为粒子挨在一起就把混合物当化合物,或把氯化钠当混合物。 ZH 对: 化合物 = 原子化学结合(NaCl)。混合物 = 没结合,可物理分开(海水)。 ZH 为何重要: 「有没有结合」是判断关键,搞反就不给分。
化合物 = 原子化学结合(NaCl)。混合物 = 没结合,可物理分开(海水)。 ZH 为何重要: 「有没有结合」是判断关键,搞反就不给分。
Reading particle diagrams
•
In a particle diagram, same-colour circles joined = one element's molecule; different-colour circles joined = a compound; separate unjoined groups = a mixture.
在粒子图里,同色圆圈连在一起 = 一种元素的分子;不同色圆圈连在一起 = 化合物;分开没连的几组 = 混合物。
✦ Exam vs Reality · 考试 vs 真实
•
"In the exam, sort matter into element / compound / mixture.
"考试时分:元素 / 化合物 / 混合物三类。
•
In real life, alloys like steel and brass sit in between — they look like compounds but count as mixtures."
真实:alloy(合金)像钢 / 黄铜算混合物,但性质像化合物。
•
"
TRY · Element / Compound / Mixture
元素 / 化合物 / 混合物
TRY
Q01Tick2 marks
Chemicals can be elements or compounds. Tick the box to show which chemicals are elements and which chemicals are compounds. The first one has been done for you. [2]
Expected answer · 标准答案
water = compound.
水 = 化合物。
Accept · 可收：
  • water = compound
  • carbon dioxide = compound
  • hydrogen = element
  • helium = element
  • magnesium = element
  • 水 = 化合物。
  • 二氧化碳 = 化合物。
Q02(a) State (b) Define3 marks
The atmosphere contains a mixture of gases. (a) What is the percentage by volume of nitrogen in clean dry air? ... [1] (b) What is the meaning of the word mixture? ........................ [2]
Expected answer · 标准答案
(a) 78%; (b) mixture = at least two substances not chemically combined; no fixed; proportion; separable by physical means.
(a) 78%; (b) mixture = at least two substances not chemically combined; no fixed; proportion; separable by physical means.
Accept · 可收：
  • (a) 78%
  • (b) mixture = at least two substances not chemically combined
  • no fixed
  • proportion
  • separable by physical means
  • (a) 78%; (b) mixture = at least two substances not chemically combined; no fixed; proportion; separable by physical means
  • 78%
  • 78 percent
Q03(a) Sort (b) Tick3 marks
Substances are either elements, compounds or mixtures. (a) Look at the list of substances. carbon copper magnesium carbonate sulfur water Sort the substances into compounds and elements. One has been done for you. [2] (b) Read these sentences about mixtures. Tick the box next to the correct sentence. Mixtures are pure substances. [ ] Mixtures can be separated by physical means. [ ] Mixtures contain only elements. [ ] Elements in mixtures are joined by chemical bonds. [ ] [1]
Expected answer · 标准答案
(a) compounds: magnesium carbonate, water; elements: carbon, copper, sulfur; (b) tick: "Mixtures can be separated by physical means.".
(a) 化合物: 镁 carbonate, 水; 元素: 碳, 铜, 硫; (b) tick: "混合物 can be separated by 物理方法."。
Accept · 可收：
  • (a) compounds: magnesium carbonate, water
  • elements: carbon, copper, sulfur
  • (b) tick: "Mixtures can be separated by physical means."
  • (a) compounds: magnesium carbonate, water; elements: carbon, copper, sulfur; (b) tick: "Mixtures can be separated by physical means."
  • (a) 化合物: 镁 carbonate, 水。
  • 元素: 碳, 铜, 硫。
  • (b) tick: "混合物 can be separated by 物理方法."。
TEST · Element / Compound / Mixture
元素 / 化合物 / 混合物
EXAM MODE · NO HINTS · 考试模式
Q04Write the correct label4 marks
(a) Mariana makes a poster showing different types of substances for a school project. The labels on her poster are missing. Write the correct label under each diagram. Choose from the following words. You can use each word once, more than once or not at all. compound element mixture [4]
Expected answer · 标准答案
(a) element; compound; mixture; element.
(a) 元素; 化合物; 混合物; 元素。
Accept · 可收：
  • (a) element
  • compound
  • mixture
  • element
  • (a) element; compound; mixture; element
  • (a) 元素。
  • 化合物。
Q05(a) Which diagram (b) Complete the sentences5 marks
Look at the diagrams. (a) Which diagram A, B, C, D or E shows: (i) One element made of single atoms? ........................ [1] (ii) One compound made of molecules? ........................ [1] (iii) A mixture of an element and a compound? ............... [1] (b) Complete the sentences to explain whether these substances are mixtures or compounds. (i) Seawater is a ........... because ........... [1] (ii) Solid sodium chloride is a ........... because ........... [1]
Expected answer · 标准答案
(a)(i) A. (a)(ii) E. (a)(iii) B; (b)(i) seawater = mixture, because salt + water / can be separated; (b)(ii) solid sodium chloride = compound, because Na and Cl joined / formula NaCl.
(a)(i) A. (a)(ii) E. (a)(iii) B; (b)(i) seawater = mixture, because salt + water / can be separated; (b)(ii) solid sodium chloride = compound, because Na and Cl joined / formula NaCl.
Accept · 可收：
  • (a)(i) A. (a)(ii) E. (a)(iii) B
  • (b)(i) seawater = mixture, because salt + water / can be separated
  • (b)(ii) solid sodium chloride = compound, because Na and Cl joined / formula NaCl
  • (a)(i) A. (a)(ii) E. (a)(iii) B; (b)(i) seawater = mixture, because salt + water / can be separated; (b)(ii) solid sodium chloride = compound, because Na and Cl joined / formula NaCl
  • (a)(i) A. (a)(ii) E. (a)(iii) B.
  • (b)(i) seawater = mixture, because salt + water / can be separated.
  • (b)(ii) solid sodium chloride = compound, because Na and Cl joined / formula NaCl.
Q06Put ticks in the table4 marks
The particle model is used to represent whether substances are elements, compounds or mixtures. Look at the particle models for different substances V, W, X, Y and Z. (a) Put ticks in the table to show which description each particle model represents. One has been done for you. [4]
Expected answer · 标准答案
V = mixture of two elements.
V = 混合物 of two 元素。
Accept · 可收：
  • V = mixture of two elements
  • W = one element
  • X = mixture of a
  • compound and an element
  • Y = mixture of two compounds
  • Z = one compound
  • V = 混合物 of two 元素。
Q07(a) Write down (b) Circle + Explain (c) Draw5 marks
Look at the particle models for elements X, Y and Z. (a) Write down two characteristics of an element. 1 ........... 2 ........... [2] (b) Element X reacts with element Z. Look at the particle model of the substance made. Circle the type of substance made. compound mixture Explain your answer. ........................ [2] (c) Draw a particle model for a mixture of elements X and Y. [1]
Expected answer · 标准答案
(a) element = one type of atom / cannot be broken down; (b) compound; reason: two elements / atom types chemically bonded; (c) drawing shows at least one X molecule and one separate Y atom; X not touching Y.
(a) element = one type of atom / cannot be broken down; (b) compound; reason: two elements / atom types chemically bonded; (c) drawing shows at least one X molecule and one separate Y atom; X not touching Y.
Accept · 可收：
  • (a) element = one type of atom / cannot be broken down
  • (b) compound
  • reason: two elements / atom types chemically bonded
  • (c) drawing shows at least one X molecule and one separate Y atom
  • X not touching Y
  • (a) element = one type of atom / cannot be broken down; (b) compound; reason: two elements / atom types chemically bonded; (c) drawing shows at least one X molecule and one separate Y atom; X not touching Y
  • (a) element = one type of atom / cannot be broken down.
Q08Draw a straight line to match3 marks
This question is about materials. Draw a straight line to match each material to its correct description. [3]
Expected answer · 标准答案
alloy = mixture containing two different metal atom types.
alloy = mixture containing two different metal atom types.
Accept · 可收：
  • alloy = mixture containing two different metal atom types
  • compound = pure substance with different atoms bonded
  • element = pure substance with same atom type
  • alloy = mixture containing two different metal atom types.
  • 化合物 = 纯物质 with different 原子 bonded。
  • 元素 = 纯物质 with 相同 原子 type。
Q09Complete the table2 marks
Look at the table showing the percentage by volume of different gases in clean, dry air. Complete the table with the name of the missing gas and the missing percentage. [2]
Expected answer · 标准答案
nitrogen 78%.
氮气 78%
Accept · 可收：

  • oxygen 21% (accept N2/O2 with correct numbers)
  • 78%
  • 78 percent
  • about 78%
  • 78 per cent

Q10Which particle model + Explain5 marks
Look at the particle models of some substances. The circles represent different types of atoms. (a) Which particle model represents a solid element? ............... [1] (b) Which particle models represent pure compounds? ............... Explain your answer. ........................ [2] (c) Which particle model represents a mixture of elements? ......... [1] (d) Which particle model represents a mixture of compounds? ........ [1]
Expected answer · 标准答案
(a) D. (b) B and E; because only two elements chemically joined / two; atom types bonded. (c) C. (d) F.
(a) D. (b) B and E; because only two elements chemically joined / two; atom types bonded. (c) C. (d) F.
Accept · 可收：
  • (a) D. (b) B and E
  • because only two elements chemically joined / two
  • atom types bonded. (c) C. (d) F
  • (a) D. (b) B and E; because only two elements chemically joined / two; atom types bonded. (c) C. (d) F
  • (a) D. (b) B and E.
  • because only two elements chemically joined / two.
  • atom types bonded. (c) C. (d) F.
Q11(a) Tick to match (b) Explain4 marks
A vacuum flask is used to keep hot drinks warm. Look at the diagram of a cross-section of a vacuum flask. Look at the particle models for different substances. (a) Tick the box to match each substance with the correct particle model. One has been done for you. [3] (b) Explain why particle model D shows a vacuum. ................... [1]
Expected answer · 标准答案
(a) steel alloy = A; air = C; pure liquid water = B; vacuum = D (given); (b) D because there is no matter / no particles.
(a) steel alloy = A; air = C; pure liquid water = B; vacuum = D (given); (b) D because there is no matter / no particles.
Accept · 可收：
  • (a) steel alloy = A
  • air = C
  • pure liquid water = B
  • vacuum = D (given)
  • (b) D because there is no matter / no particles
  • (a) steel alloy = A; air = C; pure liquid water = B; vacuum = D (given); (b) D because there is no matter / no particles
  • a space with no particles
  • a space with no matter
Q12(a)(i) State (ii) Circle (iii) Explain3 marks
Air is made up of a mixture of different substances. Fatima measures the amount of substances in the air on one day. (a)(i) Which substance is there most of? ........................ [1] (ii) Circle one substance from the pie chart that is a compound. carbon dioxide nitrogen oxygen [1] (iii) Explain how you can tell it is a compound. ................. [1]
Expected answer · 标准答案
(a)(i) nitrogen. (ii) carbon dioxide. (iii) CO2 is made of carbon and; oxygen / two different elements joined.
(a)(i) nitrogen. (ii) carbon dioxide. (iii) CO2 is made of carbon and; oxygen / two different elements joined.
Accept · 可收：
  • (a)(i) nitrogen. (ii) carbon dioxide. (iii) CO2 is made of carbon and
  • oxygen / two different elements joined
  • (a)(i) nitrogen. (ii) carbon dioxide. (iii) CO2 is made of carbon and; oxygen / two different elements joined
  • (a)(i) 氮气. (ii) 二氧化碳. (iii) CO2 is made of 碳 and。
  • oxygen / two different elements joined.
  • (a)(i) nitrogen. (ii) carbon dioxide. (iii) CO2 is made of carbon and; oxygen / two different elements joined.
Q13Name3 marks
Air contains a mixture of gases. The pie chart shows the percentages of the different gases in clean dry air. (a) Look at the pie chart. (i) Name gas A. ........................ [1] (ii) Name gas B. ........................ [1] (iii) Gas C is a mixture of gases. Name one of these gases. ...... [1]
Expected answer · 标准答案
(a)(i) nitrogen. (ii) oxygen. (iii) carbon dioxide / neon / argon /; krypton / xenon (accept formulas/symbols; name takes precedence).
(a)(i) nitrogen. (ii) oxygen. (iii) carbon dioxide / neon / argon /; krypton / xenon (accept formulas/symbols; name takes precedence).
Accept · 可收：
  • (a)(i) nitrogen. (ii) oxygen. (iii) carbon dioxide / neon / argon /
  • krypton / xenon (accept formulas/symbols
  • name takes precedence)
  • (a)(i) nitrogen. (ii) oxygen. (iii) carbon dioxide / neon / argon /; krypton / xenon (accept formulas/symbols; name takes precedence)
  • (a)(i) 氮气. (ii) 氧. (iii) 二氧化碳 / neon / argon /。
  • krypton / xenon (accept formulas/symbols.
  • name takes precedence).
Q14Complete / Name / State8 marks
Air is a mixture of gases. (a) Complete the table to show the percentage of gases in clean, dry air. [2] (b) Name one other gas that is in clean, dry air. ........................ [1] (b)(i) Magnesium sulphate is a compound. Give the name of two other compounds. ........................ [2] (b)(ii) A formula of magnesium sulphate is MgSO4. Name the three elements that combine to form this compound. .................. [2] (c) Which substance is a gas at room temperature? ........................ [1]
Expected answer · 标准答案
(a) iron, calcium, magnesium. (b)(i) calcium carbonate, water; (b)(ii) magnesium, sulfur, oxygen. (c) oxygen; (accept symbols Fe/Ca/Mg/CaCO3/H2O/Mg/S/O.).
(a) iron, calcium, magnesium. (b)(i) calcium carbonate, water; (b)(ii) magnesium, sulfur, oxygen. (c) oxygen; (accept symbols Fe/Ca/Mg/CaCO3/H2O/Mg/S/O.).
Accept · 可收：
  • (a) iron, calcium, magnesium. (b)(i) calcium carbonate, water
  • (b)(ii) magnesium, sulfur, oxygen. (c) oxygen
  • (accept symbols Fe/Ca/Mg/CaCO3/H2O/Mg/S/O.)
  • (a) iron, calcium, magnesium. (b)(i) calcium carbonate, water; (b)(ii) magnesium, sulfur, oxygen. (c) oxygen; (accept symbols Fe/Ca/Mg/CaCO3/H2O/Mg/S/O.)
  • (a) iron, calcium, magnesium. (b)(i) calcium carbonate, water.
  • (b)(ii) 镁, 硫, 氧. (c) 氧。
  • (accept symbols Fe/Ca/Mg/CaCO3/H2O/Mg/S/O.).
Q15Tick (one box per row)3 marks
Complete the table by putting a tick in the correct box under compounds, mixtures or elements. Only tick one box in each row. [3]
Expected answer · 标准答案
chemical separation = compounds.
chemical separation = compounds.
Accept · 可收：
  • chemical separation = compounds
  • physical separation = mixtures
  • one atom type = elements
  • made by chemical reaction = compounds
  • cannot be separated simpler = elements
  • keeps properties = mixtures
  • chemical separation = compounds.
Q16(a) Which diagram (b) Complete + Explain5 marks
Look at the diagrams. (a) Which diagram A, B, C, D or E shows: (i) One element made up of single atoms? ........................ [1] (ii) One compound made of molecules? ........................ [1] (iii) A mixture of an element and a compound? ............... [1] (b) Are the following substances mixtures or compounds? Explain your answer. (i) Seawater is a ........... because ........... [1] (ii) Solid sodium chloride is a ........... because ........... [1]
Expected answer · 标准答案
(a)(i) A. (a)(ii) E. (a)(iii) B; (b)(i) seawater = mixture + salt/water or physical separation; (b)(ii) sodium chloride = compound + Na/Cl chemically joined / NaCl.
(a)(i) A. (a)(ii) E. (a)(iii) B; (b)(i) seawater = mixture + salt/water or physical separation; (b)(ii) sodium chloride = compound + Na/Cl chemically joined / NaCl.
Accept · 可收：
  • (a)(i) A. (a)(ii) E. (a)(iii) B
  • (b)(i) seawater = mixture + salt/water or physical separation
  • (b)(ii) sodium chloride = compound + Na/Cl chemically joined / NaCl
  • (a)(i) A. (a)(ii) E. (a)(iii) B; (b)(i) seawater = mixture + salt/water or physical separation; (b)(ii) sodium chloride = compound + Na/Cl chemically joined / NaCl
  • (a)(i) A. (a)(ii) E. (a)(iii) B.
  • (b)(i) seawater = mixture + salt/water or physical separation.
  • (b)(ii) sodium chloride = compound + Na/Cl chemically joined / NaCl.
Q17Write the letter of the diagram3 marks
The diagrams show some arrangements of particles. Write the letter of the diagram that represents (a) molecules of a compound ........................ [1] (b) an element made up of atoms ........................ [1] (c) a mixture of different elements ........................ [1]
Expected answer · 标准答案
(a) D = molecules of compound; (b) A = element atoms; (c) B = mixture of different elements.
(a) D = 分子 of 化合物; (b) A = 元素 原子; (c) B = 混合物 of different 元素。
Accept · 可收：
  • (a) D = molecules of compound
  • (b) A = element atoms
  • (c) B = mixture of different elements
  • (a) D = molecules of compound; (b) A = element atoms; (c) B = mixture of different elements
  • (a) D = 分子 of 化合物。
  • (b) A = 元素 原子。
  • (c) B = 混合物 of different 元素。
Q18Complete the table3 marks
Substances can be elements, compounds and mixtures. Complete the table to say whether each substance is either an element, compound or mixture. [3]
Expected answer · 标准答案
air = mixture.
air = 混合物。

  • air = mixture
  • copper = element
  • salt = compound
  • steel = mixture/alloy
  • water = compound
  • air = 混合物。
  • 铜 = 元素。
Q19Draw lines1 mark
Chlorine gas, salt water and sodium chloride all contain chlorine. Draw lines from these substances to the correct type of substance. [1]

chlorine = element.
氯 = 元素。
Accept · 可收：
  • chlorine = element
  • salt water = mixture
  • sodium chloride = compound (all correct = 1)
  • 氯 = 元素。
  • 盐水 = 混合物。
  • sodium chloride = compound (all correct = 1).

Look at the diagrams. The white circles show one type of atom and the grey circles show another type of atom. [1] (c) one element made up of molecules? ........................ [1]

Q25(a) Circle (b) Name2 marks
This question is about elements, compounds and mixtures. (a) Look at the boxes. They show the particles in some substances. Which box shows a mixture of two elements? Circle the correct answer. A B C D [1] (b) A compound is made of only sodium and chlorine. What is the name of this compound? ........................ [1]
Expected answer · 标准答案
(a) A. (b) sodium chloride / NaCl.

Q26Draw one line from each type3 marks
This question is about elements, compounds and mixtures. Draw one line from each type of substance to a statement about the substance. [3]
Expected answer · 标准答案
compound = pure substance with two+ different atom types chemically joined.
compound = pure substance with two+ different atom types chemically joined.
Accept · 可收：
  • compound = pure substance with two+ different atom types chemically joined
  • mixture = two+ substances separable physically
  • element = one atom type
  • compound = pure substance with two+ different atom types chemically joined.
  • mixture = two+ substances separable physically.
  • 元素 = one 原子 type。
WRAP · Element / Compound / Mixture
元素 / 化合物 / 混合物
WRAP
📝 SUMMARY · 总结
•
Element = one type of atom.
元素 = 一种原子。
•
Compound = two+ atom types chemically joined.
化合物 = 两种以上原子化学结合。
•
Mixture = two+ substances not joined, separable physically.
混合物 = 两种以上物质没结合,可物理分开。
•
Particle diagrams: joined same-colour = element molecule;
粒子图:同色连一起 = 元素分子;不同色连一起 = 化合物;
•
joined different-colour = compound; separate groups = mixture.
分开几组 = 混合物。
•
A mixture can be separated by physical means.
混合物可以用物理方法分开。
•

但到底怎么分开它? 下一课:分离混合物。

🧠 MEMORY HOOK · 口诀金句
E-C-M：Element one, Compound joined, Mixture mixed.
记忆法：E-C-M：Element one, Compound joined, Mixture mixed。
🔁 COVER & RECALL · 盖住回想
This question is about elements, compounds and mixtures. Draw one line from each type of substance to a statement about the substance. [3]
compound = pure substance with two+ different atom types chemically joined.
compound = pure substance with two+ different atom types chemically joined.
